### Training Function Implementation

The training function will process the corpus by tokenizing the text, removing stop words, and calculating the frequency of each word in each class. It will then compute the prior probability of each class and the likelihood of each word given a class. This is done using the formula:  
$$ P(\text{class} | \text{word}) = \frac{\text{count of word in class}}{\text{total count of words in class}} $$  
$$ P(\text{class}) = \frac{\text{count of class}}{\text{total number of documents}} $$

### Prediction Function Implementation

The prediction function will take a new text and compute the probability of it belonging to each class using the trained model. It will then return the class with the highest probability. This is done by applying the Naive Bayes formula to the new text and comparing the resulting probabilities.

### Evaluation Function Implementation

The evaluation function will calculate the F1 score using the formula:  
$$ F1 = 2 \times \frac{\text{precision} \times \text{recall}}{\text{precision} + \text{recall}} $$  
This metric provides a balanced measure of precision and recall, which is essential for evaluating the performance of a text classification model.
